metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'dangling-deletion recall eval passes' with a concrete measurable criterion: name the test fixtures (e.g., 'given fixture diffs in `tests/fixtures/deletion_impact/`, `content_triggered_overlays` returns `["deletion-impact"]` for all positive cases and `[]` for all negative cases') and assert the pass condition explicitly.
findings.finding: The AC 'dangling-deletion recall eval passes' is unmeasurable and ambiguous: no test corpus is named, no recall threshold is stated, and no 'passing' criterion is defined. An executing agent cannot know when this AC is satisfied.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: 'dangling-deletion recall eval passes'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a description of the 'dangling-deletion recall eval' to the plan body: specify the test-case format (e.g., synthetic diffs with known dangling refs), the recall threshold that constitutes 'passing,' and where the eval lives (unit test, integration test, or separate benchmark).
findings.finding: AC4 requires 'dangling-deletion recall eval passes' but no part of the plan body describes what this eval consists of, how it is structured (e.g., golden test cases, a benchmark dataset, a threshold), or what 'passes' means quantitatively. This is an orphan acceptance criterion with no corresponding described deliverable.
findings.location: AC item 4

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit unit-test AC for the `overlay_union` formula extension: verify that (a) a content-triggered overlay is included in the union output, (b) a glob-triggered overlay is still included unchanged, and (c) the `already_run` subtraction in Round-B applies to content-set overlays.
findings.finding: The `overlay_union` formula extension (adding `diff_text`/`content_triggered` as a new input and unioning `content_set` into `selected`) is a changed behavior with no explicit unit-test AC; the only testing AC covers the content-trigger seam and the overlay firing predicate, not the union logic itself.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Content-trigger seam added and unit-tested; path-glob triggers unaffected.' / Data-flow section — overlay_union formula
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the testing AC to: 'overlay_union unit tests cover: content_set unioned correctly into selected; glob_set unaffected; already_run subtraction applied to content-triggered overlays in Round-B.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify where the polyglot regex lives (e.g., a configurable dict/table keyed by language or a data file) so it can be extended per-language without modifying registry.py core logic.
findings.finding: The polyglot removed-def/class/signature regex is described as 'expected to evolve per language' but no plan is stated for where it lives or how it will be updated — if hardcoded inside registry.py, evolution requires a code change with no configuration surface.
findings.location: What section: 'polyglot regex over '-' lines; the regex is expected to evolve per language'
findings.suggested_fix: Define a `DELETION_TRIGGER_PATTERNS: dict[str, str]` in a config section (or small data file imported by registry.py) mapping language identifiers to regex patterns, so `content_triggered_overlays` iterates over this table rather than embedding a single hardcoded regex.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the dangling-deletion recall eval concretely: name the fixture (a diff removing a symbol that has ≥1 surviving reference in an unchanged file), the observable expected output (overlay emits a finding citing the dangling reference), and confirm it is a behavioral test — not a snapshot or a canned-search stub.
findings.finding: The 'dangling-deletion recall eval passes' AC is under-specified: it does not state whether this is a unit test, integration test, or manual eval, nor what the oracle is, raising risk of a self-authored-oracle / snapshot anti-pattern.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'dangling-deletion recall eval passes'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Provide a concrete starter regex (e.g., `^-\s*(def |class |async def )` for Python/JS/Ruby) in the plan so the executing agent has a defined initial implementation to test against.
findings.finding: The polyglot regex for detecting removed def/class/signature lines is never specified — the plan says 'polyglot regex over `-` lines' and notes it 'is expected to evolve per language' but provides no starter pattern. An executing agent must independently decide what to implement, making this a real design choice deferred to the executor.
findings.location: What section: 'triggered when the diff removes a def/class/signature (polyglot regex over `-` lines; the regex is expected to evolve per language)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Data-flow section: `REMOVED_DEF_RE = re.compile(r'^-\s*(def |class |async def |public |private |protected |func |fn )', re.MULTILINE)` as the v1 polyglot pattern, with an explicit note that language-specific refinement is follow-on work.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether 'does not fire' in AC4 means 'does not trigger' (requiring the deterministic predicate to detect rename via paired `+` line) or 'does not produce a finding' (the agentic overlay suppresses it after triggering); update the trigger description and AC4 wording accordingly.
findings.finding: AC4 states 'a valid rename-with-callers-updated does not fire,' but the described deterministic trigger (polyglot regex over `-` lines for removed def/class/signature) would necessarily match the `-` lines of a rename diff, meaning the overlay WOULD trigger. Either the trigger must be smarter (e.g., check for a matching `+` line introducing the new name) or 'does not fire' means 'produces no finding' rather than 'does not trigger' — and the plan does not resolve which.
findings.location: AC item 4 vs. 'What' section trigger description / Data-flow section
findings.suggested_fix: If the intent is that the overlay triggers but the agentic layer finds no dangling refs and emits no finding, change AC4 to 'a valid rename-with-callers-updated does not produce a dangling-reference finding.' If the intent is the trigger itself suppresses renames, describe the paired +/- heuristic in the Data-flow section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Describe at least one positive test case (diff containing `def foo` removal triggers overlay) and one negative test case (diff that renames `foo` and updates all call sites does NOT trigger, or triggers but overlay emits no finding) as named pytest fixtures or inline examples in the plan.
findings.finding: The ACs 'Overlay fires on removed def/class/signature lines, not on unrelated diffs' and 'a valid rename-with-callers-updated does not fire' lack concrete proving commands or described test cases; the plan only mentions 'unit-tested' for the content-trigger seam but does not specify what tests cover the overlay-firing and non-firing scenarios.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria lines 3 and 4; Content-trigger seam unit-test mention

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or CLAUDE.md section capturing the content-triggered-overlay architectural decision (the `content_triggered_overlays` seam, the three-way union formula, and when to use content vs. path-glob triggers).
findings.finding: The novel content-triggered-overlay pipeline stage (the `content_triggered_overlays` seam, `diff_text` input to `overlay_union`, new union formula) is a new architectural pattern with no mention of capturing the decision in an ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design doc.
findings.location: Data-flow section and Scope

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify error handling for the deletion-impact overlay: define the failure contract (catch-and-warn, not propagate), a timeout/circuit-breaker for search_files/LLM calls, and at least one observability instrument (log line or metric) emitted on overlay invocation and on error.
findings.finding: The overlay makes LLM calls and file I/O (search_files/read_file) but the plan specifies no error handling, graceful-degradation behavior, or observability for when these operations fail — the advisory framing implies degradation but does not specify it as a reliability contract.
findings.location: What section: 'the overlay uses its read-only tools (search_files / read_file; AST/LSP when available)'; Acceptance Criteria: 'Overlay is advisory (no new BLOCK source)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the overlay spec: 'On any tool error or LLM failure, the overlay returns an empty findings list and emits a structured warning log entry (overlay_id, error_type, elapsed_ms); the review gate proceeds as if the overlay was not triggered.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether `assemble_diff` needs to emit the raw `diff_text` as a separate output (or re-derive it from `context`), since `${{ steps.assemble_diff.outputs.context }}` is a markdown composite, not the raw unified diff the content-trigger regex requires.
findings.finding: The plan's data-flow section proposes wiring `diff_text: ${{ steps.assemble_diff.outputs.context }}` into the triggers and union steps, but `assemble_diff` does NOT emit a `diff_text` output — its output schema (assemble_diff_output.schema.json) and implementation (workflow_ops.py:assemble_diff) expose only `context` (a formatted markdown composite: '## Changed files / ## Orientation / ## Diff ```diff...```') and `changed_files`. The content-triggered regex over '-' lines would receive a markdown-wrapped string, not a raw unified diff, meaning it must either account for the markdown framing or the raw diff_text must be threaded through as a separate `assemble_diff` output.
findings.location: Data-flow section: 'Wire diff_text: ${{ steps.assemble_diff.outputs.context }} into BOTH the triggers and union steps'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `diff_text` as a declared output of `assemble_diff` (emitting `dc.diff_text` or the raw unified-diff string before markdown composition), then wire `${{ steps.assemble_diff.outputs.diff_text }}` into the triggers and union steps instead of `.outputs.context`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to scope: update `test_overlay_ids_is_the_expected_closed_set` to include `deletion-impact` and update `code-review-base.md` to enumerate the new overlay (required by the no-drift guard `test_base_prompt_enumerates_exactly_the_overlay_ids`).
findings.finding: The plan does not acknowledge the existing test `test_overlay_ids_is_the_expected_closed_set` (tests/unit/test_code_review_recommend_overlays.py:21-35) which asserts the EXACT membership of OVERLAY_IDS against a hardcoded set of 11 ids. Adding `deletion-impact` to OVERLAY_IDS will break this test unless it is updated simultaneously. Similarly, the no-drift guard test `test_base_prompt_enumerates_exactly_the_overlay_ids` asserts code-review-base.md enumerates EXACTLY the OVERLAY_IDS — this test will also fail unless the base prompt is updated to enumerate the new overlay.
findings.location: AC: '`deletion-impact` added to the OVERLAY_IDS closed tuple; dispatch reaches the overlay.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly add to scope: both `round_a` and `round_b` batch-criteria lists in `gates/code-review.yaml` need a new `{ prompt: code-review-deletion-impact, when: "${{ steps.triggers.outputs.include_deletion_impact }}" }` / `when: "${{ steps.union.outputs.include_deletion_impact }}" }` entry respectively.
findings.finding: The batch criteria entries for `round_a` and `round_b` steps in `gates/code-review.yaml` are hardcoded to the existing 11 OVERLAY_IDS members (verified by reading the file lines 68-110). Adding `deletion-impact` to OVERLAY_IDS means `overlay_union` will emit an `include_deletion_impact` boolean, but neither the `round_a` nor the `round_b` batch step has a corresponding `{ prompt: code-review-deletion-impact, when: "${{ steps.triggers.outputs.include_deletion_impact }}" }` entry. The plan's scope mentions `gates/code-review.yaml wiring` but its data-flow section only explicitly calls out the `triggers` and `union` steps, leaving the batch entries unspecified.
findings.location: Scope section: 'gates/code-review.yaml wiring'; Data-flow section (addresses only triggers/union steps)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the plan's data-flow or YAML wiring section: 'Also add `{ prompt: code-review-deletion-impact, when: "${{ steps.triggers.outputs.include_deletion_impact }}" }` to round_a and `{ prompt: code-review-deletion-impact, when: "${{ steps.union.outputs.include_deletion_impact }}" }` to round_b in code-review.yaml.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-base.md` to the edit-target list and include a `- \`deletion-impact\` — <description>` bullet in the overlay catalog section.
findings.finding: `code-review-base.md` is not listed as an edit target, but adding `deletion-impact` to `OVERLAY_IDS` will break the existing no-drift guard test (`test_base_prompt_enumerates_exactly_the_overlay_ids`) which asserts that the set of `- \`<id>\` —` bullets in that file equals `OVERLAY_IDS` exactly.
findings.location: Scope section / tests/unit/test_code_review_recommend_overlays.py:148-156 / src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-base.md
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope section: `reviewers/code-review-base.md (add deletion-impact bullet to the overlay catalog)`. The bullet must match the `^- \`([a-z0-9-]+)\` —` pattern the no-drift guard regex expects.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/test_code_review_recommend_overlays.py` to the edit-target list and update `test_overlay_ids_is_the_expected_closed_set` to include `deletion-impact` in the expected set.
findings.finding: `test_overlay_ids_is_the_expected_closed_set` (tests/unit/test_code_review_recommend_overlays.py:20-35) hardcodes the exact 11-member set and will fail when `deletion-impact` is added to `OVERLAY_IDS`; this test file is not listed as an edit target.
findings.location: Scope section / tests/unit/test_code_review_recommend_overlays.py:20-35

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `{ prompt: code-review-deletion-impact, when: \"${{ steps.triggers.outputs.include_deletion_impact }}\" }` to `round_a` AND `{ prompt: code-review-deletion-impact, when: \"${{ steps.union.outputs.include_deletion_impact }}\" }` to `round_b` in `gates/code-review.yaml`, and document these as explicit edit targets.
findings.finding: The `round_a` and `round_b` batch criteria lists in `gates/code-review.yaml` are unenumerated consumers: each has one `{ prompt: code-review-<id>, when: ... }` entry per `OVERLAY_IDS` member, so adding `deletion-impact` requires new entries in both batch steps — but the plan does not mention these edits.
findings.location: gates/code-review.yaml lines 67-111 / Scope section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `content_triggered_overlays` will scan `assemble_diff.outputs.context` or a distinct raw `diff_text` output; if the latter, add `assemble_diff` output schema (`assemble_diff_output.schema.json`) and `workflow_ops.py:assemble_diff` to the edit-target list.
findings.finding: The data-flow section proposes wiring `diff_text: ${{ steps.assemble_diff.outputs.context }}` into the `triggers` and `union` steps, but `assemble_diff`'s output schema only exposes `context` (a formatted kernel context string) and `changed_files` — there is no raw `diff_text` output field. The content-trigger regex needs to scan raw `-` lines of a unified diff, not the formatted context.
findings.location: Data-flow section / src/rebar/schemas/assemble_diff_output.schema.json / src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py:55

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `{ prompt: code-review-deletion-impact, when: "${{ steps.triggers.outputs.include_deletion_impact }}" }` to the `round_a` batch criteria list AND `{ prompt: code-review-deletion-impact, when: "${{ steps.union.outputs.include_deletion_impact }}" }` to the `round_b` batch criteria list in `gates/code-review.yaml`; include this in the YAML wiring scope of the plan.
findings.finding: The data-flow section only specifies wiring `diff_text` into the `triggers` and `union` overlay_union steps in code-review.yaml, but it omits the equally load-bearing change: adding a `{ prompt: code-review-deletion-impact, when: "${{ steps.triggers.outputs.include_deletion_impact }}" }` entry to the `round_a` batch criteria list and a matching entry to the `round_b` batch criteria list. Without these two YAML entries, `include_deletion_impact` is computed by `overlay_union` but never read, and the overlay never actually runs — the entire trigger mechanism is silently inert.
findings.location: Data-flow section / gates/code-review.yaml round_a and round_b batch criteria lists
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the Scope and Data-flow sections to explicitly call out that `code-review.yaml`'s `round_a` and `round_b` batch criteria lists each need a new entry for `deletion-impact` (mirroring the existing 11 overlay entries), in addition to the `diff_text` wiring into `triggers` and `union`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reword AC4 to accurately reflect that the trigger FIRES on a rename (because the `-def` line matches) but the overlay produces no findings when all callers are updated, and that this is the expected/acceptable over-firing behavior the plan explicitly sanctions.
findings.finding: AC4 states 'a valid rename-with-callers-updated does not fire,' but the described deterministic trigger (polyglot regex over `-` lines for removed def/class/signature) necessarily fires on any rename because a rename always produces a `-def old_name(` removal line. The overlay then searches UNCHANGED files and finds no dangling callers (they were updated in changed lines), so it produces no findings — but the trigger still fires and the overlay still runs. The AC wording directly contradicts the plan's own 'over-firing costs one advisory pass, never a wrong verdict' statement and could lead an implementer to treat an empty-findings invocation on a clean rename as a test failure or a mechanism bug.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC4's last clause to: 'a valid rename-with-callers-updated fires the trigger but the overlay produces no dangling-reference findings (acceptable over-firing per the advisory design).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Justify why the diff-line trigger uses a new bespoke polyglot regex rather than the existing T1 (universal-ctags / tree-sitter) grounding machinery already present in the project, or explain the tradeoff (e.g., diff-context vs file-context, no repo-root access at trigger time).
findings.finding: The plan's polyglot regex for detecting removed def/class/signature lines is a reimplementation of symbol-detection logic the project already has in its T1 grounding layer (ADR 0030 names 'universal-ctags / tree-sitter syntactic' as the T1 floor for polyglot symbol lookup), yet the plan does not explain why the existing T1 machinery is not used for the diff trigger rather than a new bespoke regex.
findings.location: What section — 'deletion-impact overlay (agentic, findings-only, one-hop): triggered when the diff removes a def/class/signature (polyglot regex over "-" lines)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a brief note to the 'Data-flow' or 'What' section: if the trigger runs at diff-assembly time without a repo root (pure text pass), state that explicitly as the reason the T1 oracle can't be used here. If a repo root IS available, consider calling the existing T1 layer instead of adding a new regex.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the 'AST/LSP when available' assertion: either (a) confirm what tools are actually exposed to the deletion-impact agentic step and update the spec accordingly, or (b) clarify that 'AST/LSP' refers only to the T2 opt-in path and document the fallback behavior when it is absent (the overlay relies on search_files only).
findings.finding: 'AST/LSP when available' is stated as part of the caller-search mechanism but is an unverified capability assertion: existing agentic overlays only expose read_file/search_files tools, and the T2 LSP backend (pyright) is default-off and opt-in per ADR 0030 — it is unclear whether the deletion-impact overlay step will actually have access to AST or LSP tooling beyond the standard file tools.
findings.location: What section — 'Caller-search mechanism (specified): the overlay uses its read-only tools (search_files / read_file; AST/LSP when available)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `reviewers/code-review-base.md` to the Scope section and specify a `- \`deletion-impact\` — ...` bullet must be added to its overlay catalog to satisfy the existing no-drift guard test (`test_base_prompt_enumerates_exactly_the_overlay_ids`).
findings.finding: The plan specifies adding `deletion-impact` to `OVERLAY_IDS` and creating `reviewers/code-review-deletion-impact.md`, but never mentions updating the overlay catalog in `code-review-base.md`. An existing no-drift guard test asserts that the prompt enumerates EXACTLY `OVERLAY_IDS` — adding to `OVERLAY_IDS` without updating `code-review-base.md` will break this test.
findings.location: Scope section / AC: `deletion-impact` added to OVERLAY_IDS
findings.suggested_fix: Add `reviewers/code-review-base.md` to the Scope and include an AC sub-bullet: 'code-review-base.md catalog updated with deletion-impact entry; no-drift guard passes.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that `gates/code-review.yaml` requires adding `{ prompt: code-review-deletion-impact, when: \"${{ steps.triggers.outputs.include_deletion_impact }}\" }` to `round_a` and `{ ..., when: \"${{ steps.union.outputs.include_deletion_impact }}\" }` to `round_b` batch blocks.
findings.finding: The `code-review.yaml` gate hardcodes all overlay prompts as individual rows in both `round_a` and `round_b` batch blocks. Adding `deletion-impact` to `OVERLAY_IDS` means `overlay_union` will emit `include_deletion_impact`, but neither batch block will have a `{ prompt: code-review-deletion-impact, when: ... }` row — so the overlay can never actually execute.
findings.location: Scope section: `gates/code-review.yaml wiring` / Data-flow section
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the gates/code-review.yaml wiring description to call out both batch-block row additions explicitly, in addition to the triggers/union diff_text wiring.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the fallback contract for `content_triggered_overlays(diff_text)` when `diff_text` is None/empty (should return `[]`), and clarify whether `assemble_diff` must emit a raw `diff_text` output or whether the enriched `context` string is the intended input for the regex.
findings.finding: The `content_triggered_overlays(diff_text)` function has no specified fallback when `diff_text` is absent or empty — which is the normal case for git-range invocations where `assemble_diff` receives `base`/`head` refs instead of a pre-built diff. The analogous `glob_triggered_overlays(changed_files=[])` naturally returns `[]`; the new function must define the same empty-input contract.
findings.location: Data-flow section: `registry.content_triggered_overlays(diff_text) -> list[overlay_id]`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the plan that `reviewers/code-review-deletion-impact.md` must declare `outputs: code_review_findings` (reusing the existing overlay output schema) and name the `criteria` dimension string that deletion-impact findings should carry (e.g. `\"deletion-impact\"`), so `threshold_for` routing and the no-drift guard can be wired correctly.
findings.finding: The `deletion-impact` overlay prompt's output schema (`outputs:` frontmatter field) and the `criteria` dimension string its findings must carry are never specified. Every existing overlay hardcodes `outputs: code_review_findings` and a specific `criteria` value (e.g. `[\"security\"]`). These are load-bearing: `threshold_for` routes by criteria string; the no-schema case causes silent fallback to the default findings model.
findings.location: Scope section: `reviewers/code-review-deletion-impact.md`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly specify that `diff_text` is an optional parameter (defaulting to `''` or `None`) in `overlay_union` so existing callers are unaffected, and document this as the expand-contract choice; or, if a required parameter is intended, add an explicit migration note listing all callers to update atomically.
findings.finding: The plan modifies the `overlay_union` op by adding a new `diff_text` input parameter, but does not specify whether this parameter is optional (with a default) or required. If required, any existing caller of `overlay_union` that does not pass `diff_text` will break silently or with an error, and no rollback path or expand-contract sequence is described for this interface change.
findings.location: Data-flow section — 'Add a `diff_text` (or precomputed `content_triggered`) input to the `overlay_union` op'
findings.suggested_fix: Make `diff_text` optional with a default of `''` (empty string), so `content_triggered_overlays('')` returns `[]` and the union formula degrades gracefully to the existing glob-only behavior for any caller not yet updated.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate all existing callers of `overlay_union` (tests, other YAML gates, wrapper functions) and confirm each is updated or explicitly noted as unaffected by the new `diff_text` parameter.
findings.finding: The plan extends `overlay_union`'s interface by adding a new `diff_text` (or `content_triggered`) input parameter, but only names one call site (the YAML `union` step) that must be updated. Any other callers of `overlay_union` — including existing tests, other gate YAML files, or wrapper functions — would break silently or require signature changes that the plan does not enumerate.
findings.location: Data-flow section: 'Add a diff_text (or precomputed content_triggered) input to the overlay_union op'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a subsection under Scope or the Data-flow section listing every call site of `overlay_union`; if the YAML step is the only caller, state that explicitly. If making `diff_text` optional (defaulting to `''`/no content triggers), document that default so existing callers remain valid without changes.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether a second concrete content-triggered overlay (beyond `deletion-impact`) is planned in this epic or the next; if not, consider inlining the regex logic directly into `overlay_union` (where the glob call already lives) and skipping the standalone `content_triggered_overlays` function until Rule-of-Three is met.
findings.finding: `content_triggered_overlays(diff_text)` is a new standalone function with exactly one current caller (the `deletion-impact` trigger path inside `overlay_union`). The existing analogous function `glob_triggered_overlays` itself has only 2 production call-sites (workflow_ops.py line 82 and registry.py line 250), below the Rule-of-Three threshold. Extracting the content-trigger logic into its own top-level registry function rather than inlining it into `overlay_union` (where the glob logic already lives) is premature abstraction unless a second content-triggered overlay is concretely planned.
findings.location: Data-flow section: `registry.content_triggered_overlays(diff_text) -> list[overlay_id]`
findings.suggested_fix: In `overlay_union`, replace the proposed `content_set = registry.content_triggered_overlays(diff_text)` call with an inline `content_set = registry._content_triggered_overlays_impl(diff_text)` (private helper) or simply inline the one-line regex match directly, deferring the public function extraction until a second content-triggered overlay requires it.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
